evals.prompt: I have a SPIP plugin `mon_plugin` with a filtre function `mon_plugin_nettoyer_url` in `inc/mon_plugin_filtres.php`. I want to add PHPUnit unit tests for it without installing a full SPIP. How do I set it up?
evals.expected_output: Explains self-contained composer setup at plugin root. Shows tests/bootstrap.php with if(!function_exists()) mocks, require_once of the plugin file, use of $GLOBALS['_test_*'] injection, composer tests-unit script, and tests/unit/ directory structure.

assertions.description: Uses require_once to load the plugin file directly (not find_in_path or include_spip)
Explains tests/bootstrap.php as the place for lightweight mock stubs
Shows or mentions if (!function_exists()) guards in the mock bootstrap
Mentions $GLOBALS['_test_*'] pattern for controlling mock return values in tests
Mentions `composer tests-unit` script or equivalent phpunit command for running unit tests
Mentions tests/unit/ as the directory for unit test classes

evals.prompt: My SPIP plugin has a CVT form with a `verifier` function that calls `sql_countsel`. I want to write integration tests that hit a real database. How do I install the SPIP test environment with spip-cli and write the test?
evals.expected_output: Explains composer install-spip-test, scripts/install-spip-test.sh with spip-cli commands (core:telecharger, core:preparer, core:installer SQLite3, plugins:activer), tests/bootstrap_integration.php loading vendor/spip/spip, and a concrete integration test example using real sql_countsel.

assertions.description: Mentions spip-cli commands for setting up SPIP (core:telecharger, core:preparer, or core:installer)
Mentions `composer install-spip-test` or scripts/install-spip-test.sh as the setup entry point
Mentions tests/bootstrap_integration.php as the integration bootstrap file
Mentions that SPIP is installed inside vendor/spip/spip/ (not a global install)
Mentions SQLite3 as the test database engine
Warns that tests/bootstrap.php (unit mocks) must NOT be included in the integration bootstrap

evals.prompt: I've added a custom `#MON_PLUGIN_STATUS` balise to my SPIP plugin. I want to test it with PHPUnit — it should output 'actif' when the config is set and be empty otherwise. How do I do that?
evals.expected_output: Explains SquelettesTestCase from spip/tests, assertEqualsCode / assertEmptyCode assertions, that this needs the integration tier (real SPIP loaded), and shows a concrete test class extending SquelettesTestCase.

assertions.description: Mentions the SquelettesTestCase class by name
Shows assertEqualsCode or assertEmptyCode (or similar SquelettesTestCase assertion method)
Clarifies that SquelettesTestCase tests require real SPIP (integration tier, not unit tier)
Shows a concrete PHP class extending SquelettesTestCase
Mentions spip/tests package as the source of SquelettesTestCase
